2019-12.text: COVID-19 begins to affect cross-boundary travel; Christmas

2020-01.text: From Jan 30: CFT, XRL WK, HHS, MFT, STK, TMFT, MKT suspend passenger services; New Year

2020-02.text: From Feb 4: LMC, LMCSL, LW, MFT suspend; Feb 5: Kai Tak Cruise Terminal suspends; CNY

2020-06.text: LMCSL briefly resumes for cross-boundary students; Dragon Boat Festival

2020-08.text: HYW Heung Yuen Wai opens (freight only)

2021-06.text: TMFT Tuen Mun Ferry Terminal permanently ceases cross-boundary passenger service; Dragon Boat Festival

2021-07.text: Kai Tak Cruise Terminal briefly resumes cruise-to-nowhere service

2021-12.text: HYW & LMCSL set up special clearance for LegCo election voting; Christmas

2022-01.text: Kai Tak Cruise Terminal cruise-to-nowhere service halted

2022-03.text: Mainland medical team arrives via HYW Heung Yuen Wai (special clearance)

2023-01.text: New Year; From Jan 7: CFT, LMCSL, MFT, MKT resume; Jan 14: XRL WK resumes; Jan 17: Kai Tak resumes

2023-02.text: CNY; Feb 6: Full Shenzhen-HK reopening; LW, LMC, HYW fully resume; quota removed

2023-09.text: MKT Man Kam To suspends passenger & cargo clearance; Mid-Autumn Festival

2023-10.text: MKT Man Kam To gradually resumes cargo service; National Day

2023-11.text: MKT Man Kam To fully resumes passenger & cargo clearance

2023-12.text: All COVID restrictions lifted; cross-boundary travel fully normalised; Christmas

2024-07.text: HHS Hung Hom checkpoint permanently closed
